zh.Woodsome Hall, Huddersfield, West Yorksh: 英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾德斯費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Drakes於1337年出生在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾德斯費爾德時，他的父親Thomas Drakes30歲，母親Lucy Mayfield26歲。他於大約1366年與Christian Falos在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1389年在他的家鄉享年52歲。

zh-hans.Woodsome Hall, Huddersfield, West Yorksh: 英国英格兰韋斯特 越可爾德斯費爾德

nameMeanings.name: Drakes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Drake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: habitational name from Drax (Yorkshire). The placename probably derives from Old English dræg ‘portage, place where boats are dragged’ or dræg ‘drag net’, influenced by Old Norse drag ‘portage’, with a plural drages being shortened to drags, Drax.
In some cases perhaps an Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) cognate in some other language.

variants.name: Drake
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English drake, either ‘drake, male duck’ (compare Duck ) or ‘dragon’ (Old English draca ‘snake, dragon’ or the cognate Old Norse draki), including an emblematic dragon on a flag (compare Dragon ). Both the Old English and the Old Norse forms are from Latin draco ‘snake, monster’; its sense as a nickname is unclear but it may have had the sense ‘standard bearer’. The name was taken to Ireland in the 13th century and reinforced by later English settlers in the 17th century.
German: from Low German drake ‘dragon’, familiar as image on signboards, hence a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn with such signboard.
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized and Flemish, of Draak, a cognate of 2 above, from draak (Middle Dutch drake) ‘dragon’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Drakes was born in 1337, in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Drakes, was 30 and his mother, Lucy Mayfield, was 26. He married Christian Falos about 1366, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1389, in his hometown, at the age of 52.

Il a épousé Christian Falos environ 1366, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1389, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 52 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Drakes(이)가 1337년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 허더스필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Drakes(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucy Mayfield(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1366년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Christian Falos(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1389년 그의 고향에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.
